stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Sui Sin Far (1865-1914) was a Chinese English writer.
  • Throughout her career, she wrote pioneering short fiction about Chinese American communities.
  • Her collection Mrs. Spring Fragrance was published in 1912.
  • It is considered one of the first books of fiction by an author of Chinese descent published in North America.
stem: The student wants to introduce Sui Sin Far to an audience unfamiliar with her career. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Sui Sin Far is the author of Mrs. Spring Fragrance, a collection published in 1912.
Published in 1912, Mrs. Spring Fragrance is a collection of short fiction by Sui Sin Far.

Chinese English writer Sui Sin Far wrote pioneering short fiction about Chinese American communities, including one of the first books of fiction by an author of Chinese descent published in North America, Mrs. Spring Fragrance.
Mrs. Spring Fragrance by Sui Sin Far is considered an important early work of Chinese American fiction.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The sentence introduces Sui Sin Far to an audience unfamiliar with her career, identifying her as a pioneering Chinese English writer of fiction about Chinese American communities and noting a career accomplishment.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes that Sui Sin Far wrote a collection but doesn't introduce her to an audience unfamiliar with her career. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence introduces Sui Sin Far's collection; it doesn't introduce Sui Sin Far. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence explains that a collection written by Sui Sin Far is an important early work; it doesn't introduce Sui Sin Far herself to an audience unfamiliar with her career.
